triton_current_actual_source_boundary.read: This is the current apples-to-apples Triton source-boundary gate: q, dO, KV, indices, output, and lse in; dQ and dKV out. The full-minus-sink split is the approximate global dKV accumulation wall.

tilelang_route_owned_fused_source_bounda.read: The 16.67 ms benchmark event and 17.00 ms rocprof average are the same generated TileLang dQ+dKV source-boundary kernel measured two ways. This is the closest TileLang fused equivalent measured so far, and it loses to the 11.13 ms Triton gate.

selected_limit_materialized_or_hsk_input.read: These are useful dKV reducer/body surfaces, but they are not slices of the 16.67 ms fused source-boundary kernel. They depend on easier/materialized inputs or omit source-boundary dQ/recompute work.

two_kernel_pdp_then_dkv_rejected.read: A P/dP producer followed by the TileLang dKV consumer does not materialize the apparent dKV-only win; the HBM slab boundary and route spill erase it.

non_additivity_warning.invalid_composite: Triton no_dkv ~2.03 ms + TileLang dKV-only ~5.97-6.90 ms
why_invalid: The TileLang 5.97-6.90 ms dKV numbers are not operating on the same live source-boundary state as the Triton no-dKV kernel.
They do not include the source-boundary recompute and dQ ownership costs that make the measured fused TileLang route-owned kernel 16.67 ms.
The measured two-kernel P/dP producer plus dKV consumer is already 16.2-19.8 ms, so the apparent 8 ms hybrid is not materialized.

next_kernel_contract: consume q, dO, compressed/shared KV, LI indices, output, and lse directly
preserve the Triton query-major q/dO and row-term reuse
add true lane/warp/block segmented dKV accumulation before global memory
avoid materialized P/scaled-dP slabs and avoid route-owned recompute that repeats QK/PV per D block
compare against the 11.13 ms full source-boundary sparse-attention backward gate
